commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: Run auth init in background to prevent 1-min page load delay

Previously, DOMContentLoaded awaited AuthModule.init() which calls
supabase.auth.getSession() - a network request. Users with cached
sessions had to wait ~1 minute for Supabase to respond before
the home screen rendered.

Auth now runs in background (non-blocking). Router.init() and
HomeScreen render immediately. Auth UI updates asynchronously
once session is confirmed.

commit.message:
security(hardening): add request body size limit and embedding model validation

Prevents two real attack vectors: (1) CWE-400 memory exhaustion via oversized
POST bodies that bypass Pydantic validation and rate limiting — new middleware
rejects requests exceeding MAX_BODY_BYTES (64KB default) before buffering.
(2) CWE-73 path traversal/SSRF via poisoned EMBEDDING_MODEL env var — startup
validation rejects filesystem paths, URLs, and shell metacharacters. 280 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve prediction popup latency by caching domain settings and removing tabs.get pre-flight (#291)

Two performance bottlenecks were causing ~15-25 ms of avoidable overhead on
every keystroke prediction:

1. `resolveDomainRuntimeSettings` was called on every prediction request,
   performing 5 parallel chrome.storage reads (~5-15 ms) even though domain
   settings virtually never change between keystrokes.
   Fix: introduce `DomainSettingsCache` (500 ms TTL, per-domain key) in
   `MessageRouter`.  The cache is immediately invalidated when
   `CMD_OPTIONS_PAGE_CONFIG_CHANGE` is received so settings changes still
   take effect without waiting for expiry.

2. `BackgroundServiceWorker.runPrediction` called `chrome.tabs.get` before
   every `sendMessage` as a tab-existence pre-flight, adding ~5-10 ms of
   extra IPC latency.  `sendMessage` already throws when the tab/frame is
   gone, so the pre-flight was redundant.
   Fix: call `chrome.tabs.sendMessage` directly and catch the error.

Performance gain per keystroke: ~15-25 ms less background-side overhead.
50 consecutive predictions for the same domain now incur 1 storage read
instead of 250.

Tests added:
- `tests/DomainSettingsCache.test.ts` — 11 unit tests covering cache hit/miss,
  TTL expiry, per-domain isolation, invalidation, and a latency benchmark
  asserting cached requests are ≥5× faster than uncached ones.
- Two regression tests in `tests/background.routing.test.ts`:
  - Verifies `chrome.tabs.get` is NOT called during prediction dispatch.
  - Verifies the domain settings cache is invalidated after a config change
    so the next prediction sees fresh language/settings values.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Modernize build system and security

Security improvements:
- Add helmet middleware for security headers
- Add HSTS header to nginx config
- Update Express 4.13 to 4.21
- Update jQuery 2.1 to 3.7 (security patches)

Build system modernization:
- Migrate Gulp 3 to Gulp 4 (rewrite gulpfile.js)
- Update Babel 5 to Babel 7 with @babel/preset-env
- Replace gulp-uglify with gulp-terser for modern JS
- Remove deprecated gulp-browserify
- Update vinyl-source-stream and vinyl-buffer

Dependency updates:
- body-parser, cookie-parser, morgan, compression
- babelify 6 to 10
- del 5 for Gulp 4 compatibility

Vulnerabilities reduced from 97 to 56 (42% reduction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cart bar hiding Complete Sale button - CSS class mismatch

**Problem:**
- Component used className="sticky-cart-bar" but CSS defined ".rs-cart-bar"
- Cart bar had NO styling, pushing footer below visible area
- Users couldn't access Complete Sale button on desktop

**Solution:**
- Changed className from "sticky-cart-bar" to "rs-cart-bar"
- Updated all child classes to match CSS (rs-cart-summary, rs-cart-icon, etc.)
- Now cart bar has proper sticky positioning (z-index: 50, bottom: 0)
- Footer is now visible with Complete Sale button

commit.message:
Add disease brain regions section with CSS styling

- Adds 15 disease cards organized by category (Anxiety, OCD-Related,
  Mood, Psychotic, Neurodevelopmental, Neurodegenerative, Alcohol-related)
- Each card shows implicated brain regions as color-coded pills using a
  10-region anatomical color system

commit.message:
Add Supabase integration for cloud persistence

- Add @supabase/supabase-js client with graceful fallback to localStorage
- Create unified storage layer (src/lib/storage.ts) with batch load/save
- Update App.tsx and RadiologyTemplatesPage to use new storage layer
- Add SQL schema for Supabase app_data table (key-value document store)
- Add .env.example documenting required environment variables

When VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are set, data syncs
to Supabase with localStorage as offline cache. Without them, the app
works exactly as before using localStorage only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.7.0 — audit enhancements, brew-upgrade, apply-defaults

security-audit.sh:
  - Bluetooth state check via system_profiler (finding: Medium)
  - Developer mode check via DevToolsSecurity (finding: Medium)
  - World-readable files scan: ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.gnupg, ~/.config/op (finding: High)
  - Sudoers NOPASSWD detection (finding: High)
  - New sections: Sensitive File Permissions, Sudoers Configuration

brew-upgrade.sh (mac-deploy-upgrade):
  - brew update → captures outdated list → brew upgrade → brew cleanup
  - Logs dated upgrade summary to private/machines/<hostname>/brew-upgrades.log
  - --dry-run shows outdated packages without installing
  - --no-casks skips cask upgrades

apply-defaults.sh (mac-deploy-defaults):
  - Screen lock: require password immediately, 5-minute screensaver
  - Screenshots: move to ~/Documents/Screenshots, disable shadows
  - Finder: show extensions, hidden files, full path in title bar
  - AirDrop: contacts only
  - Mail: disable remote image loading (tracking pixel protection)
  - Safari: no auto-open downloads, fraud warnings, full URL bar
  - Software Update: auto-check, auto-download, security data (sudo)
  - --dry-run prints each defaults write command without applying

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
